architecture_fit.note: Minimal delta from base_model self-attention (L2-distance kernel, RoPE removed); attention-only, single layer, content-matching solves the alignment task with path_overlap=1.0.

baseline_comparison.note: Clear strawmen: diagonal positional head decays 1.0->0.41 across warp while the content head holds at 1.0; benchmark also tracks diagonal and uniform baselines.

faithfulness.note: Hand-built and transparent by construction, and head contrast isolates the L2 kernel, but README does not explicitly propose a causal/ablation check as the rubric requests.

operating_range.note: Holds robustness=1.0 across the full fixed warp sweep (0->0.75) but never probes higher noise or extreme warp, so no breaking point is shown.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Fully hand-set circuit with no training that reproduces the target alignment behavior exactly — exactly what this bonus rewards.

visual_judgement.note: Per-head heatmaps with ground-truth warp path + argmax overlay plus an overlap-vs-warp line chart make the 'tracks warp, not diagonal' claim legible at a glance.

visualisation_rationale.note: 'Why this visualisation' ties the heatmap (band bends along warp) and line chart (overlap y-axis vs warp x-axis, baselines on same axes) directly to the alignment_robustness metric.

notes: A clean, correct hand-built content-matching head that solves the warp-alignment task perfectly (path_overlap=1.0, alignment_robustness=1.0) as a minimal delta from base_model with a well-designed diagonal strawman and a clear two-view visualisation. It loses ground on faithfulness (no proposed causal check for the synthetic setup) and operating range (never stress-tested to a breaking point). Mean of scored items ~4.4 -> good.
